Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: AI systems that take action and make decisions over multiple steps without constant human guidance, often acting like autonomous agents.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Retrieval‑Augmented Generation; combining language models with databases, the model retrieves domain‑specific facts before generating answers.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: When an AI model generates outputs that are factually wrong or inconsistent with its training data, often presented confidently.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: A class of generative models that create images or other data by iteratively “denoising” random noise, commonly used in image generators.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Models that can process and reason over multiple modalities (e.g., text, images, audio) within a single system.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback; train model outputs with human preferences (e.g., ranking responses), aligns LLMs with human values.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Human-on-the-Loop; AI acts autonomously while humans monitor and only intervene during anomalies or high-risk cases (e.g., AI spam filtering).
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Built‑in constraints or filters that limit an AI model’s behavior to reduce harmful or unsafe outputs.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: In‑Context Learning; LLM learns a task from a few examples included directly in the input prompt, without updating its internal weights.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: A unit of text (e.g., a word, subword, or symbol) into which input is split before being processed by a language model.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: AI models that run locally on devices (phones, sensors, cars) rather than in the cloud, enabling low‑latency, private inference.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Explainable AI; AI systems designed so that their decisions can be understood and interpreted by humans, improving transparency and trust.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Training a smaller “student” model to mimic the behavior of a larger “teacher” model, often for efficiency and deployment on edge devices.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: A mechanism (e.g., EigenLayer) to re‑use assets already staked on one blockchain (like ETH) to secure additional protocols, earning extra yield.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Users interact with a single wallet while the underlying system routes transactions across many chains, hiding chain‑specific complexity.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Intent-Based System; users decide intent (e.g., “swap at best price”) and smart contracts/relayers optimize across chains, DEXs, and liquidity pools.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: zk-Rollup; Layer‑2 scaling solution that batches transactions off‑chain and posts zero‑knowledge proofs to the base layer for security/finality.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Self‑Sovereign Identity; digital identity model where users control credentials and selectively disclose info without needing centralized providers.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Intent-Based DeFi; users define their financial goals (e.g., “grow my portfolio by 10% over 6 months”) and smart agents execute this across protocols.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Quantum Driven Reinforcement Learning for Scalable Blockchain Transaction Processing; protocol using cryptography against quantum computer attacks.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Decentralized Identity; unique identifier on‑chain or in a decentralized ledger, enables verifiable credentials/login without centralized platforms.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Tokenized Real‑World Assets (e.g., bonds, real estate, commodities) to enable fractional ownership and programmable finance.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: MEV Bot/Searcher; scans pending transactions for Miner (or Maximal) Extractable Value, often via arbitrage, frontrunning, or back‑running.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Self-executing program stored on-chain that runs with predetermined terms, removing intermediaries and risk with code-based "if/then" logic.
Incorrect, this is tech slang! Definition: Decentralized Autonomous Organization; a member-owned entity governed by blockchain code rather than hierarchical management.
